title: JsonRpcDispatcher
inputs: Parsed JSON-RPC envelopes (request, notification, response); method handlers; send callback
outputs: Dispatched handler results; outbound requests with auto-incrementing IDs; notifications
guarantees: Requests get monotonically incrementing integer IDs; pending map tracks all outbound requests; reject_all_pending clears all pending on transport close; dispatch routes by envelope shape (method+id=request, method-only=notification, id+result/error=response)
error_semantics: MethodNotFoundError (-32601) for unregistered methods; TesseronError in handler produces matching error response; other exceptions produce InternalError (-32603); send failure closes transport

title: WebSocketTransport
inputs: WebSocket connection with tesseron-gateway subprotocol; JSON text frames
outputs: Parsed JSON-RPC envelopes; connection lifecycle events
guarantees: Binds to loopback only (127.0.0.1 or ::1); advertises tesseron-gateway subprotocol; rejects upgrades without correct subprotocol; accepts exactly one connection; one JSON-RPC envelope per text frame; binary frames coerced to UTF-8; deletes manifest on close
error_semantics: Connection errors propagate as TransportClosedError; subprotocol mismatch rejects upgrade

title: UdsTransport
inputs: Unix domain socket connection; NDJSON-framed lines
outputs: Parsed JSON-RPC envelopes; connection lifecycle events
guarantees: Creates private directory mode 0o700 under system temp; binds socket inside private directory; chmod 0o600 on socket file; accepts exactly one connection; NDJSON framing (one envelope per newline); empty lines ignored; cleans up socket/directory/manifest on close

title: SessionStateMachine
inputs: Transport events (open, close); protocol messages (welcome, claimed)
guarantees: States: DISCONNECTED→HANDSHAKING→AWAITING_CLAIM→CLAIMED→CLOSED; transport open triggers HANDSHAKING; welcome triggers AWAITING_CLAIM; claimed triggers CLAIMED; transport close from any state triggers CLOSED; on CLOSED: reject pending requests, fire cancellation signals, clean up subscriptions, drop progress, reject in-flight sample/confirm/elicit
error_semantics: TransportClosedError on all pending operations when entering CLOSED

title: HandshakeManager
inputs: App metadata; action/resource manifests; capabilities; transport
outputs: WelcomeResult with sessionId, capabilities intersection, claimCode, resumeToken
guarantees: tesseron/hello sent as first message after transport open; protocolVersion is 1.2.0; app.id matches /^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$/; reserved app IDs rejected; on tesseron/claimed: update cached agent identity, clear claimCode, overwrite capabilities if agentCapabilities present
error_semantics: ProtocolMismatchError (-32000) on major version mismatch from gateway

title: ActionRegistry
inputs: Action declarations (name, input/output schemas, handler, annotations, timeoutMs); actions/invoke requests
outputs: InvocationResult with invocationId and output; actions/list_changed notifications
guarantees: Input validated against Pydantic model before handler runs; handler does not run on validation failure; strict_output validates return value against output schema; dynamic registration/removal triggers actions/list_changed with full updated manifest; requiresConfirmation annotation carried through to MCP
error_semantics: InputValidationError (-32004) with validation issues on input failure; HandlerError (-32005) on strict output failure or handler exception; ActionNotFoundError (-32003) for unknown action

title: ResourceManager
inputs: Resource declarations (name, description, subscribable, handler); resources/read, resources/subscribe, resources/unsubscribe requests
outputs: ResourceReadResult; subscription acknowledgment; resources/updated notifications; resources/list_changed notifications
guarantees: Cleanup function called on unsubscribe; cleanup function called for all subscriptions on transport close; subscription map cleared on close; dynamic registration/removal triggers resources/list_changed with full manifest

title: ProgressCancellation
inputs: actions/progress params (invocationId, message, percent, data); actions/cancel notification; timeoutMs config
guarantees: Progress percent should increase monotonically; on actions/cancel: fire cancellation signal for invocation, return CancelledError; abort after timeoutMs (default 60000), return TimeoutError; race handler against timeout/cancel so agent gets response immediately; orphaned handler may continue but agent is not blocked
error_semantics: CancelledError (-32001) on cancel; TimeoutError (-32002) on timeout

title: SamplingBridge
inputs: SamplingRequestParams (invocationId, prompt, schema, maxTokens); capability check
outputs: SamplingResult with content; parsed/validated value when schema provided
guarantees: Checks ctx.agent_capabilities.sampling before calling; when schema provided: parse content as JSON, validate against schema; gateway enforces maxSamplingDepth=3
error_semantics: SamplingNotAvailableError (-32006) when agent lacks sampling capability; SamplingDepthExceededError (-32008) when depth exceeds 3; HandlerError (-32005) on parse/validation failure of sampling response

title: ElicitationBridge
outputs: ElicitationResult with action (accept/decline/cancel) and optional value; True/False for confirm; validated value or None for elicit
guarantees: ctx.confirm sends empty-object schema, returns True on accept, False on decline/cancel/no-capability; ctx.elicit validates schema before sending (object type, primitive properties, no combinators); ctx.elicit throws on no-capability (no safe default); permissive fallback schema when no explicit schema provided
error_semantics: ElicitationNotAvailableError (-32007) from ctx.elicit when agent lacks elicitation; InvalidParamsError (-32602) when schema violates MCP constraints

title: CapabilityNegotiation
inputs: App-declared capabilities; welcome capabilities intersection; agentCapabilities from tesseron/claimed
outputs: Authoritative capability set for handler context
guarantees: App declares all four capabilities as true in hello; welcome.capabilities is the intersection; on tesseron/claimed with agentCapabilities: overwrite capabilities; handlers trust the authoritative set; graceful degradation: sampling throws, confirm returns False, elicit throws, subscriptions never called, progress silently dropped
error_semantics: SamplingNotAvailableError and ElicitationNotAvailableError for capability-gated operations

title: ErrorModel
inputs: Exception instances (TesseronError subclasses, other exceptions); incoming JSON-RPC error responses
outputs: JSON-RPC error responses; rejected pending requests with TesseronError instances
guarantees: Typed error classes for all 12 protocol error codes; TesseronError in handler maps to JSON-RPC error with matching code/message/data; other exceptions map to HandlerError (-32005); incoming JSON-RPC errors construct TesseronError and reject pending request; error.data carries structured payloads (validation issues, depth info)
error_semantics: All error codes from -32700 to -32011 have corresponding typed classes

title: DiscoveryManifest
inputs: App metadata; transport binding info (ws URL or uds path)
guarantees: Manifest version is 2; instanceId uses inst- prefix; discovery directories created with mode 0o700; manifest files written with mode 0o600; manifest deleted on graceful shutdown; cleanup on SIGINT and SIGTERM; claim breadcrumb files mode 0o600
error_semantics: File permission errors propagate; stale manifest detection via pid field

title: SessionResume
inputs: Stored sessionId and resumeToken; app metadata; current actions/resources/capabilities
outputs: WelcomeResult with rotated resumeToken; resumed session state
guarantees: tesseron/resume sent with stored credentials and current manifest; new resumeToken persisted (overwriting stored token); on success: session retains claimed status, no new claimCode needed; on failure: clear stored credentials, fall back to fresh tesseron/hello; SDK re-subscribes to resources after successful resume
error_semantics: ResumeFailedError (-32011) on unknown session, bad token, TTL elapsed, cross-app resume, unclaimed zombie

title: TypeDefinitions
inputs: Raw JSON dicts from wire; Python values for serialization
outputs: Validated Pydantic model instances; serialized JSON dicts
guarantees: All wire types modeled as Pydantic BaseModel with ConfigDict(extra=ignore); camelCase wire names aliased to snake_case Python names; JSON-RPC envelope shapes: Request (method+id), Notification (method only), SuccessResponse (id+result), ErrorResponse (id+error); id may be string, int, or None
error_semantics: Pydantic ValidationError on malformed input (caught by dispatcher)

title: ActionContext
inputs: Current invocation state; agent identity; capabilities; transport send callback
outputs: Progress notifications; sampling/elicit/confirm results; log entries
guarantees: ctx.signal fires on timeout or cancel; ctx.agent returns agent identity; ctx.agent_capabilities returns authoritative capability set; ctx.progress emits fire-and-forget notification; ctx.sample sends sampling/request and awaits response; ctx.confirm sends elicitation with empty schema; ctx.elicit sends elicitation with validated schema; ctx.log emits structured log notification
error_semantics: Capability-gated operations raise appropriate errors; TransportClosedError on operations after close

title: PyodideWebSocketTransport
inputs: WebSocket URL; browser-native WebSocket via Pyodide FFI (js.WebSocket + pyodide.ffi.create_proxy)
outputs: Parsed JSON-RPC envelopes via asyncio.Queue; connection lifecycle events
guarantees: Same interface as WebSocketClientTransport; uses browser-native WebSocket API via Pyodide FFI; tesseron-gateway subprotocol passed to WebSocket constructor; one JSON-RPC envelope per message (REQ-011); binary data coerced to UTF-8 (REQ-015); create_proxy instances stored and destroyed on close to prevent memory leaks; bridges event-driven JS callbacks to asyncio via Queue.put_nowait and Event.set
error_semantics: TransportClosedError on connection failure or unexpected close
